---
title: "Credentials"
---

Before you run the application, prepare the local credential file.

## Create the `.env` File

In your local copy of the repository, create a file named `.env` in the root folder. This file will hold the credentials for connecting to the SQL Server database.



Replace the placeholder values (foo) with the actual credentials provided for your SQL Server instance. The file should look like this:

```bash
MSSQL_SERVER=foo
MSSQL_DATABASE=foo
MSSQL_USERNAME=foo
MSSQL_PASSWORD=foo
APP_USER=foo

MSSQL_DRIVER=ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server
SQL_ENCRYPT=yes
TRUST_SERVER_CERTIFICATE=true
```

Make sure to keep the `.env` file secure and do not share it with others, as it contains sensitive information.

Dont forget to add `.env` to your `.gitignore` file if you are using Git, to prevent it from being accidentally committed to version control.


:::{.callout-warning}
Do not hard-code database passwords in `app.py`, `sql_example.py`, or repository files. In enterprise projects, this is considered a serious security mistake.
:::
